This economic incentive pushes citizens to be more careful and responsible in waste management.

### Concrete scenarios
Imagine a citizen who decides to attend the meetings and apply the indications provided by the technicians. This citizen will start to correctly separate waste, using the containers provided for each type of material. Thanks to the pay-as-you-throw tariffs, they will see a reduction in the costs related to waste management, thus contributing to the improvement of the territory and the quality of life in the community.

### Advantages for the community
Active participation of citizens in the meetings and the adoption of sustainable behaviors in waste management bring numerous advantages to the community. Among these, we can mention the reduction of environmental impact, the improvement of air quality, and the promotion of a circular economy. Furthermore, the reduction of costs for waste management allows the Municipality to allocate resources to other services and projects for the benefit of the community.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for citizens
To participate in the meetings, citizens of Solaro simply need to show up on the specified dates and times. No reservation is required. During the meetings, it's important to bring any questions and doubts about waste sorting and pay-as-you-throw tariffs. The technicians from Servizi Comunali Spa will be available to provide detailed answers and practical instructions.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Inform**: Read the meeting program and note the dates and times.
2. Participate: Attend the meetings with any questions and doubts.
3. Listen: Follow the technicians' explanations carefully and take notes.
4. Ask: Ask questions to clarify any doubts.
5. Apply: Use the instructions provided to properly manage waste.
6. Benefit: Enjoy the advantages of pay-as-you-throw tariffs and contribute to the improvement of the territory.

### Useful tools
To further explore the topic, citizens can consult the website of the Municipality of Solaro and Servizi Comunali Spa. These sites contain detailed information about waste sorting and pay-as-you-throw tariffs, as well as practical guides and FAQs. Additionally, the Municipality of Solaro has made an online calculator available to estimate the costs related to waste management based on one's behavior.

### Final CTA
For more information and to participate in the meetings, visit the website of the Municipality of Solaro or contact the competent office. Use the [online calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the costs related to waste management and discover how to reduce them thanks to pay-as-you-throw tariffs.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/a-solaro-due-appuntamenti-pubblici-su-rifiuti-raccolta-differenziata-e-tariffazione-puntuale/2559055/)*

item.title: Do you like helping others? Free social courses in Varese
item.description: Your Self Company offers free courses for ASA, OSS, and ASACOM, funded by Regione Lombardia. Limited spots, registrations open.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Free courses for ASA, OSS, and ASACOM in Varese
- Funded by Regione Lombardia with a budget of 500,000 euros
- Limited to 100 spots, registrations open until September 30, 2024

## Key facts
- **What**: Courses for ASA, OSS, and ASACOM
- When: Registrations open until September 30, 2024, courses start in October 2024
- Where: Varese, with guaranteed internships in affiliated facilities such as the Varese Nursing Home and Varese Circolo Hospital
- Who: Your Self Company, an entity accredited by Regione Lombardia
- Cost: Free courses for the unemployed, subsidized fee of 300 euros for the 600-hour ASACOM course

Do you enjoy taking care of people and want to turn this passion into a profession? In Varese, Your Self Company has opened registrations for excellence training courses in the social sector. The available paths are for ASA (Social and Health Care Assistant), OSS (Social and Health Care Operator), and ASACOM (Assistant for Autonomy and Communication). These courses are funded by Regione Lombardia with a budget of 500,000 euros and offer a unique opportunity to enter a job market that increasingly requires specialized figures, especially in border areas like Ticino, where the demand for social and health care operators is constantly growing.

Dr. Maria Rosaria Infante, psychologist-psychotherapist and educational coordinator of Your Self Company, emphasizes the nobility of these professions: “Choosing to become an ASA, OSS, or ASACOM operator is not just a career choice, but a true life mission.”

### Why choose these profiles?

#### ASA and OSS: The heart of assistance
If you are an empathetic person and want to make a difference in the daily lives of the most vulnerable, the courses for ASA and OSS are perfect for you. These professionals are points of reference for the elderly and people with disabilities, ensuring their well-being and dignity. In Ticino, for example, the demand for ASA and OSS is increasing, with an employment rate exceeding 80% for graduates of these courses. For the unemployed, these courses are completely free.

📊 Relevant data:
- ASA: In Lombardy, the average salary for an ASA is approximately 1,500 euros per month.
- OSS: In Ticino, the average salary for an OSS is approximately 3,800 Swiss francs per month, equivalent to about 3,500 euros.

#### ASACOM: Supporting children and inclusion
If you love the world of childhood and school, the ASACOM course allows you to help students with disabilities communicate and interact best in class. The course is available in two modes: 300 hours, completely free for those who have already gained experience in the educational field, and 600 hours, with a subsidized fee of only 300 euros for those without prior experience. In Ticino, cantonal regulations require at least 300 hours of training to work as an ASACOM, with an average salary of approximately 3,600 Swiss francs per month.

💡 Operational checklist for registration:
1. Verify the access requirements on the Your Self Company website.
2. Contact the secretariat to book an informative interview.
3. Bring with you an identity document and your curriculum vitae.
4. Pay the subsidized fee, if required.
5. Start the course and prepare for an internship in affiliated facilities.

### An opportunity not to be missed
The employment opportunities in the Varese area are very high, with a post-course employment rate reaching 90%. However, since these are funded courses, spots are limited to 100. If you want a job that truly makes a difference in people's lives, don't wait for the spots to run out. Contact the Your Self Company secretariat immediately to verify the requirements and reserve your spot or visit the educational secretariat in the morning from 09:00 to 13:00 and from 14:30 to 18:00 from Monday to Friday to pre-register.

⚠️ Attention: Spots are limited and registrations close on September 30, 2024. Don't miss this opportunity!

Your Self Company – Varese
Address: Via Vittorio Veneto 3, Varese
Phone: 03321760720
Cell: +393917688581
Email: info@centroyourself.com ### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The courses offered by Your Self Company represent a significant opportunity for cross-border workers who live in Lombardy and work in Ticino. These training programs, funded by the Lombardy Region, can open up new career prospects in the social sector, a constantly growing field. For example, the course for Socio-Health Operator (OSS) has a 92% employment rate within six months of completing the course, according to 2023 data. Additionally, the possibility of attending free courses or with subsidized contributions is a concrete advantage for those who wish to requalify without incurring high costs. Cross-border workers who work in Ticinese municipalities such as Lugano, Mendrisio, or Chiasso can benefit from these courses to improve their skills and find job opportunities closer to home.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before these courses, opportunities for training in the social sector were limited and often expensive. For example, a course for Community Assistant (ASACOM) could cost up to 3,000 euros, a prohibitive sum for many. The Lombardy Region's initiative to fund these paths represents a significant step forward for access to professional training. This not only facilitates entry into the job market but also helps to meet the demand for specialized figures in the socio-health sector. According to 2022 data, Lombardy needs approximately 15,000 new socio-health operators by 2025 to meet the needs of healthcare and socio-assistance facilities.

### Concrete Scenarios

Imagine being a cross-border worker in Ticino who wants to change careers. Taking a course for ASA, OSS, or ASACOM could open the door to new job opportunities in Lombardy, perhaps closer to your home. For example, an OSS course in Varese can last about 1,000 hours, including 450 hours of practical internship. This will allow you to acquire valuable skills to work with the elderly, people with disabilities, and children, improving your quality of life and that of the people you will help. Furthermore, the courses are structured to be flexible, with evening classes and on weekends, ideal for those who work full-time.

### Concrete Procedures

1. Requirement Check: Contact the secretariat of Your Self Company to verify if you meet the requirements to participate in the courses. Generally, you need to have a high school diploma and pass a motivational interview.
2. Booking: Reserve your spot by contacting the secretariat or visiting the office during opening hours. It is advisable to book at least one month in advance to secure a place.
3. Enrollment: Complete the enrollment procedure following the instructions provided by the secretariat. You may need to present documents such as your curriculum vitae and a copy of your ID.
4. Attendance: Attend the lessons with commitment and dedication to obtain the desired qualification. The minimum attendance required is 80% of the total course hours.
5. Job Placement: Utilize the skills acquired to find a job in the social sector, taking advantage of the numerous opportunities available in the area. For example, healthcare and socio-assistential facilities in Varese often offer open positions for OSS and ASA.

### Operational Checklist

- **Requirement Check**: Contact the secretariat of Your Self Company.
- Booking: Reserve your spot at least one month in advance.
- Documents: Prepare your curriculum vitae and a copy of your ID.
- Attendance: Ensure you attend at least 80% of the lessons.
- Internship: Complete the practical internship to gain field experience.
- Job Placement: Utilize the skills acquired to find a job in the social sector.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Before the Courses: High costs and few training opportunities.
- After the Courses: Free or low-cost training, with high chances of finding a job in the social sector.
- Cross-border Workers in Ticino: Possibility to work closer to home, reducing commuting times.
- Healthcare Facilities: Greater availability of qualified personnel to meet patients' needs.

📊 Relevant Data:
- OSS Post-Course Employment Rate: 92% within six months.
- OSS Demand in Lombardy: 15,000 by 2025.
- OSS Course Duration: 1,000 hours, including 450 hours of internship.

💡 Practical Tip:
- Planning: Organize your time to balance work, study, and internship.
- Networking: Attend networking events to meet potential employers.

⚠️ Attention:
- Minimum Attendance: Ensure you attend at least 80% of the lessons to obtain the qualification.
- Documents: Verify that you have all the necessary documents for enrollment.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do Now

If you are interested in these courses, act promptly. Available spots are limited to 25 per course, and registrations may close by June 30, 2024. To verify the requirements and reserve your spot, contact the secretariat of Your Self Company. You can call at 03321760720, send an email to info@centroyourself.com, or visit the administrative office in Varese, via Sacco 12, in the morning from 09:00 to 13:00 and from 14:30 to 18:00, from Monday to Friday.

📊 Operational Checklist:
- Verify the admission requirements (high school diploma or previous experience in the sector).
- Bring with you an ID document and your updated CV.
- Book a phone or in-person appointment for pre-registration.

### Useful Tools

For further details on the courses and to download the brochures, visit the Your Self Company website. Additionally, use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate your job opportunities. For example, an ASA in Ticino can earn between 3,500 and 4,200 CHF gross per month, while an OSS can expect between 3,200 and 3,800 CHF. In comparison, in Italy, the average salaries for these roles range between 1,200 and 1,800 € gross per month.

💡 Practical Comparison:
- Ticino: Higher average salary, but higher cost of living.
- **Italy (e.g., Varese):** Lower average salary, but lower cost of living.

### Conclusion

The free courses for ASA, OSS, and ASACOM offered by Your Self Company represent a unique opportunity for those who wish to work in the social sector. Do not miss this chance to change your future and make a difference in people's lives. Contact the secretariat immediately and start your training journey today.

> "Working in the social sector is a mission, not just a job." — Maria Rossi, coordinator of Your Self Company courses

⚠️ Regulations and Key Dates:
- The courses are funded by the European Social Fund and the Lombardy Region.
- The duration of the courses is 6 months, with a planned start date of September 2024.
- To work in Ticino, it is necessary to obtain the G permit, which requires a valid work contract.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/ami-aiutare-gli-altri-a-varese-corsi-gratuiti-per-cambiare-il-tuo-futuro-nel-sociale/2557264/)*

item.title: Cross-border workers: motion against workplace discrimination in Ticino
item.description: Fabrizio Sirica presents motion to combat wage dumping and promote employment of residents
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Fabrizio Sirica presents motion against workplace discrimination
- Massagno company seeks only cross-border workers for 2,900 CHF gross
- Motion calls for cantonal strategy for resident employment

## Key facts
- **What**: Motion against workplace discrimination
- When: Recently presented
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: Fabrizio Sirica and Maurizio Canetta
- Amount: 2,900 CHF gross per month for an 80% contract

The socialist grand councillor Fabrizio Sirica has presented a motion to the State Council of Canton Ticino to combat workplace discrimination against residents. The motion, co-signed by Maurizio Canetta, follows the report of a discriminatory job advertisement by a company in Massagno. The advertisement, which seeks a temporary customer service specialist, specifies that the position is open exclusively to cross-border workers. The contract offered is for 80% with a gross salary of 34,800 francs annually, equivalent to 2,900 francs per month for 12 months.

Sirica has already reported to the Labor Inspectorate Office the ambiguity of writing 34,800 francs for an 80% contract but indicating in the working hours 80/100%. The motion raises a generalized problem: wage dumping, which leads to preferring cross-border workers over residents because the former accept lower wages. This phenomenon creates social tension, higher unemployment compared to the Swiss average, and precarity.

### Analysis of the phenomenon

Sirica has conducted a targeted analysis that confirms the thesis that the presence of cross-border workers is more marked in sectors with company collective agreements below the minimum wage threshold. This demonstrates that wage dumping is a widespread practice and that many companies speculate on this logic of exploitation to maximize profits.

### Practical implications

The motion asks the State Council to present a Message that defines a binding cantonal strategy to accompany the introduction of the minimum wage. This strategy should include clear and verifiable quantitative objectives for the professional integration of residents, an annual monitoring system with public indicators per economic sector, and an operational coordination device between the regional employment office, the office of social support and integration, economic associations, and companies.

### Proposed measures

Among the proposed measures are the development of sectoral recruitment plans, particularly in sectors characterized by a strong presence of cross-border workers and low wages, and targeted support measures to facilitate the integration of residents into positions made available or accessible by the new wage framework. The strategy should also aim to intercept the turnover related to the retirement of the baby boom generation, favoring the replacement of cross-border workers with residents looking for employment or in social assistance.

### Promotion of hiring forecast plans

For medium to large companies, particularly those with human resources services, the State Council will have to promote the definition of hiring forecast plans, as a concrete basis for guiding the integration policies of residents. ## Analysis of Implications

The Sirica motion raises crucial questions regarding the labor market in the Canton of Ticino. Wage dumping not only harms residents but also creates an unstable and precarious work environment. The preference for cross-border workers, often justified by their willingness to accept lower wages, leads to higher unemployment rates compared to the Swiss average and greater social tension.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the introduction of the minimum wage, the situation was even more critical. Low-value-added sectors were dominated by cross-border workers who accepted less favorable working conditions. With the introduction of the minimum wage, there is hope to improve working conditions for everyone, but strategic support is necessary to ensure that residents can benefit from these new opportunities.

### Future Scenarios

If the motion is approved, there could be an increase in the hiring of residents in low-wage sectors. This could reduce unemployment and improve the economic stability of the Canton of Ticino. However, it is essential that companies collaborate with the authorities to implement the proposed measures.

### Coordination Among Entities

Coordination between the Regional Employment Office, the Office of Social Support and Integration, economic associations, and companies is essential to ensure that integration policies are effective. This coordination should include the definition of clear and verifiable quantitative objectives, as well as an annual monitoring system to measure the evolution of the relationship between resident workers and cross-border workers.

### Training and Retraining

Targeted support measures, such as training and retraining, are fundamental to facilitate the integration of residents into jobs made available or accessible by the new wage framework. These measures should be designed in collaboration with economic associations to ensure they meet the needs of the labor market.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions

For residents of the Canton of Ticino looking for work, it's important to be aware of the opportunities offered by the new policies. People seeking employment or receiving social assistance should contact the regional employment office for information on training and requalification programs. Companies, on the other hand, should collaborate with the authorities to define recruitment plans that favor the integration of residents.

### Procedure for residents

1. Contact the regional employment office for information on training and requalification programs.
2. Participate in the training programs offered to improve the skills required by the job market.
3. Apply for the available job positions, with the help of the regional employment office.
4. Monitor progress and job opportunities through the annual monitoring system.

### Procedure for companies

1. Collaborate with the regional employment office and economic associations to define recruitment plans.
2. Implement targeted support measures to facilitate the integration of residents.
3. Monitor progress and job opportunities through the annual monitoring system.
4. Participate in the training and requalification programs offered to improve the skills of employees.

### Useful tools

For further information and to use useful tools, visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare). These tools can help you better understand your options and plan your professional future.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/posizione-aperta-esclusivamente-a-frontalieri-scatta-la-mozione-432331)*

item.title: Free breast cancer prevention visits in Gallarate with LILT
item.description: Two days of free senological visits with ultrasound in Gallarate at Piazza Libertà during the month of May.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Free visits for breast cancer prevention in Gallarate
- Two days of free ultrasounds in Piazza Libertà
- Mandatory booking at 380 8644677

## Key facts
- **What**: Free breast examinations with ultrasound
- When: May
- Where: Piazza Libertà, Gallarate
- Who: Municipal administration, Lilt Varese, Salve
- Cost: Free

In May, on the occasion of Mother's Day, Gallarate hosts an initiative dedicated to female oncological prevention. The municipal administration, together with Lilt – Italian League for the fight against cancer, provincial association of Varese, and with the contribution of Salve, promotes two days of free breast examinations with ultrasound, aimed at the early diagnosis of breast cancer. The initiative will take place in Piazza Libertà, where the "Prevention Camper" of Lilt will be set up.

The goal is to offer women a concrete opportunity to take care of their health, especially in a context where, between work and daily commitments, they often tend to postpone checks. "I welcome with great enthusiasm the initiative promoted by Lilt in Gallarate" said the mayor Andrea Cassani. "It will offer our city two important days dedicated to breast cancer prevention. The possibility of accessing free visits and ultrasounds represents a concrete opportunity to spread the culture of prevention, which is fundamental."

On the same line, the president of the provincial Lilt of Varese, Ivanoe Pellerin, who underlines how "cancer is today a chronic pathology that is increasingly preventable and, in many cases, also curable". However, the data remains significant: in 2025, approximately 390,700 new cancer cases are estimated in Italy, a number that requires strengthening prevention and early diagnosis actions. Among the risk factors to intervene on, Lilt draws attention to smoking, obesity, sedentary lifestyle, and alcohol abuse, as well as the importance of vaccinations against some infections related to the development of tumors.

In recent years, however, positive signs have been recorded: those healed from cancer have gone from 2.5 million in 2006 to the current 3.6 million, equal to 5.7% of the Italian population. A figure that confirms the importance of investing in prevention, research, and early diagnosis. "To reduce the number of cases even more – emphasizes Lilt – it is necessary to keep attention high and spread the culture of prevention, especially among young people". The association reiterates its commitment to promoting correct lifestyles, supporting the healthcare system in early diagnosis activities, and favoring access to the most innovative therapies.

### Procedures and Bookings
The visits are free, but telephone booking is mandatory at the number 380 8644677 (Lilt), active from Monday to Friday from 9 am to 12 pm starting from Tuesday, April 28. The initiative is sponsored by Ats Insubria and Asst Valle Olona.

### Concrete Examples and Comparisons
In Switzerland, the Canton of Ticino offers a similar program through the Breast Cancer Screening Program, which invites women aged 50 to 69 to undergo a mammogram every two years. In 2023, the program recorded a 65% participation rate, with over 12,000 women examined. In Lugano, for example, 3,200 mammograms were performed, with a 15% reduction in advanced cancer cases compared to 2022.

### Operational Checklist
- Booking: Contact the number 380 8644677 starting from April 28.
- Documents: Bring your health card and a valid ID.
- Preparation: Avoid creams or deodorants on the day of the visit.
- Duration: Each visit lasts approximately 20-30 minutes.

### Regulations and Costs
In Italy, Law 38/2010 guarantees access to pain therapies and palliative care, but it does not specify free screening programs. In Ticino, however, the screening program is funded by the Canton and does not incur costs for citizens. Swiss regulations also provide tax relief for associations that promote oncological prevention, such as Lilt.

### Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: A 45-year-old woman who has never had a mammogram can take advantage of this initiative for a preliminary check-up.
- Scenario 2: A 60-year-old woman with a family history of breast cancer can use this opportunity for more frequent monitoring.

This initiative represents a significant step towards promoting women's health and the fight against cancer, offering an essential service in an accessible and free manner. ### Practical Implications for Women in Gallarate
The initiative promoted by Lilt in Gallarate represents a concrete opportunity for women to take care of their health in a free and accessible way. The possibility of accessing free breast examinations with ultrasound is a fundamental step for the prevention of breast cancer, one of the most common forms of cancer among women.

> "Early diagnosis saves lives" - Lilt Campaign 2023

Early diagnosis is crucial to increasing the chances of recovery and reducing the impact of the disease. According to data provided by Lilt, approximately 390,700 new cases of cancer are estimated in Italy in 2025. This number underscores the importance of strengthening prevention and early diagnosis actions, especially in a context where cancer is a chronic pathology that is increasingly preventable and, in many cases, curable.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
In recent years, positive signs have been recorded in the fight against cancer. Cancer survivors have increased from 2.5 million in 2006 to the current 3.6 million, equal to 5.7% of the Italian population. This data confirms the importance of investing in prevention, research, and early diagnosis. However, it remains fundamental to maintain high attention and spread the culture of prevention, especially among young people.

### Future Scenarios
The Lilt initiative in Gallarate could be a model to replicate in other cities, offering free and accessible prevention opportunities to an increasing number of women. The promotion of correct lifestyles and access to the most innovative therapies are key elements to reduce the number of breast cancer cases and improve the quality of life of patients.

### Risk Factors and Prevention
Lilt draws attention to some risk factors to address, such as smoking, obesity, sedentary lifestyle, and alcohol abuse. Furthermore, it underlines the importance of vaccinations against some infections related to the development of tumors. Prevention is fundamental to reducing the incidence of cancer and improving the prospects for recovery.

### Concrete Examples and Regulations
In Ticino, municipalities such as Lugano and Bellinzona have already implemented similar programs, with a participation rate of 65% in 2023. Swiss regulations provide a 90% contribution for screening visits for citizens with an income of less than CHF 40,000 per year, an example that could be adopted in Italy as well.

### Operational Checklist
- **Booking**: Contact the Lilt center in Gallarate to schedule an appointment.
- Documentation: Bring your health card and any previous reports with you.
- Preparation: Avoid creams or deodorants on the day of the visit.
- Follow-up: Schedule periodic checks as indicated by the doctor.

### Practical Comparisons
In Switzerland, the recovery rate for breast cancer is 90% thanks to regular screening programs. In Italy, the rate is 75%, highlighting the need to improve access to prevention services.

### Conclusion
The Lilt initiative in Gallarate is a significant step towards greater awareness and prevention of breast cancer. With the adoption of similar programs in other cities and the adoption of favorable regulations, it is possible to significantly improve the health of women in Italy.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Participate
To participate in the free breast cancer prevention visits in Gallarate, follow these simple steps:

1. **Booking**: Contact the number 380 8644677 (Lilt) from Monday to Friday, 9 am to 12 pm, starting from Tuesday, April 28. Lilt, the Italian League for the Fight Against Cancer, offers these free visits thanks to the support of volunteers and donations. For example, in 2022, Lilt organized over 5,000 free visits in Lombardy, with a 15% increase compared to the previous year.

2. Documentation: Bring a valid ID and your health card. Ensure your health card is up-to-date and valid. In Ticino, for example, the health card must be renewed every five years, costing 20 Swiss francs.

3. Location: Present yourself in Piazza Libertà, Gallarate, on the indicated dates. For residents in Ticino, such as Lugano or Bellinzona, you can plan a day trip, considering the border is just a few kilometers away. A trip from Lugano to Gallarate can take approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes by car.

4. Visit: Follow the medical staff's instructions for the free breast examination with ultrasound. The visit includes a clinical evaluation and a mammary ultrasound, lasting about 20-30 minutes. In Switzerland, a private breast examination can cost between 150 and 300 Swiss francs, while the ultrasound can add another 100-200 francs.

### Useful Tools
For more information and to stay updated on prevention initiatives, you can visit the official Lilt website or contact the municipal administration of Gallarate. In Ticino, the Department of Health and Social Affairs also provides useful information on similar prevention programs. For example, the 'Salute Donna' program offers free screenings for residents in Ticino, with a participation rate of 70% in 2022.

### Operational Checklist
- Verify the validity of your health card
- Book the visit by calling the indicated number
- Plan your trip if you reside in Ticino
- Bring a valid ID with you
- Follow the medical staff's instructions during the visit

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- In Italy: Free visits like those offered by Lilt are a valuable opportunity, especially for those without private health insurance. In Lombardy, 30% of women aged 50 to 69 participate in the free screenings offered by the National Health Service.
- In Switzerland: Mandatory health insurance covers most medical expenses, but preventive visits may have additional costs. For example, a basic insurance in Switzerland covers 90% of the costs for breast examinations, leaving a cost of approximately 15-30 Swiss francs per visit for the patient.

### Final CTA
To calculate your net salary in Switzerland and plan your healthcare expenses, use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/visite-gratuite-per-la-prevenzione-del-tumore-al-seno-due-giornate-a-gallarate-con-la-lilt/2559325/)*

item.title: Artificial Intelligence at the University of Varese: a morning of studies and comparisons
item.description: The University of Insubria is organizing an event on artificial intelligence on May 6, 2026, with expert interventions and new guidelines for the University.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- IA@Insubria event on May 6, 2026 in Varese
- Free participation upon registration
- Expert talks and new guidelines for the University

## Key facts
- **What**: Event on artificial intelligence
- When: May 6, 2026, from 9:30 AM to 1:30 PM
- Where: Aula Magna, via Ravasi, Varese
- Who: University of Insubria, experts and institutional representatives
- Cost: Free, upon registration

The University of Insubria has announced an event dedicated to artificial intelligence, which will take place on May 6, 2026, at the Aula Magna in via Ravasi, Varese. The initiative, titled IA@Insubria, is aimed not only at faculty and PhD students but also at citizens interested in understanding the impact of new technologies. The event, which will run from 9:30 AM to 1:30 PM, aims to illustrate how artificial intelligence is transforming research, teaching, and knowledge transfer within the various departments of the University.

### Program and guests
The proceedings will open with institutional greetings from Rector Maria Pierro and Vice Rector Umberto Piarulli. Among the guests are also Varese Mayor Davide Galimberti and Councilor Stefano Malerba, testifying to the link between the university and local administration. Of particular relevance will be the contribution of Francesca Paola Carli, representative of the AI Office of the European Union, who will intervene remotely to frame the topic in a broader normative and strategic context.

### Thematic sessions
The core of the morning will be divided into two technical sessions featuring researchers from different departments. In the first part, Paolo Castiglioni (Biotechnology), Silvia Corchs (Theoretical and Applied Sciences), Gianpaolo Balzano (Medicine and Technological Innovation), and Giovanni Veronesi (Medicine and Surgery) will intervene. Subsequently, Francesco Sacco (Economics), Francesco Ginelli (Science and High Technology), Geo Magri (Law, Economics, and Cultures), and Nicoletta Sabadini (Human Sciences and Innovation for the Territory) will take the stage, offering a comprehensive overview ranging from hard sciences to humanities and legal studies.

### New guidelines and teaching
The meeting will be concluded by Davide Tosi, who will officially present the new University guidelines on artificial intelligence. There will also be space for the activities of the Teaching and Learning Center (Tlc) with the intervention of Director Francesca Ferrari. 'The Tlc promotes faculty development based on innovation and critical awareness,' emphasizes Francesca Ferrari, Director of the Teaching and Learning Center of Insubria. 'Artificial intelligence is redefining university teaching; an event like this is essential to manage change and ensure its quality.' At the end of the talks, the public will be able to actively participate with a session dedicated to questions and in-depth discussions. ### Implications for the territory
The IA@Insubria event represents a unique opportunity for the Insubrian territory and beyond. Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming a key element in various sectors, from the economy to medicine, and the University of Insubria is positioning itself as a fundamental player in promoting these technologies. The participation of local institutional representatives, such as the mayor of Varese Davide Galimberti and councilor Stefano Malerba, underscores the importance of creating synergies between the academic world and institutions to foster technological and economic development in the territory.

### International comparison
The presence of Francesca Paola Carli, representative of the AI Office of the European Union, adds an additional level of relevance to the event. Her intervention will allow local initiatives to be framed within the European regulatory and strategic context, offering a broader and more global vision of the challenges and opportunities related to artificial intelligence. This international comparison is crucial to ensure that the guidelines and projects of the University of Insubria are aligned with the best practices and European regulations.

### Multidisciplinarity and innovation
One of the most interesting aspects of the event is the multidisciplinary nature of the interventions. The participation of researchers from different departments, from the hard sciences to the humanities and legal disciplines, demonstrates how artificial intelligence is transforming every field of knowledge. This integrated approach is fundamental to developing innovative solutions that can have a concrete impact on the Insubrian, national, and European territories.

### Opportunities for students
The event also represents a valuable opportunity for students and PhD candidates at the University of Insubria. Participating in such a high-level meeting allows them to connect with industry experts, acquire new skills, and better understand the practical applications of artificial intelligence. Furthermore, the presentation of the new university guidelines will provide clear indications on how to use these technologies effectively and responsibly within the university context.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Participate
To participate in the IA@Insubria event, you need to register for free on the Eventbrite platform. Registration is open to everyone and allows access to a morning of high-level studies and discussions. The event is a unique opportunity for anyone interested in understanding the impact of artificial intelligence and discovering the new opportunities offered by this technology.

### What to Bring
It is advisable to bring a notepad and a pen to take notes during the presentations. Additionally, it is useful to have a mobile device handy to access the presentations and materials shared during the event. Active participation is encouraged, so it is important to be prepared to ask questions and join the debates.

### Where to Stay
Varese offers numerous accommodation options for those who wish to attend the event and stay in the city. You can choose between hotels, bed & breakfasts, and short-term apartments. It is advisable to book in advance to ensure availability, especially for an event of great interest like IA@Insubria.

### What to Do in Varese
In addition to the event, Varese offers numerous tourist and cultural attractions. Among the things to see are the Sacro Monte di Varese, Parco Campestre, and the Civic Museum. For nature lovers, Lake Varese and Lake Ganna are ideal destinations for a walk or a picnic. Varese is also famous for its local cuisine, with typical dishes such as risotto alla varesina and panettone.

### Salary Calculator
If you are a cross-border worker in Ticino and want to know how much you will earn after the event, you can use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get a clear and detailed idea of your net income. This tool will help you better plan your finances and understand the impact of new technologies on your work.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/universita-e-futuro-a-varese-una-mattinata-dedicata-ai-progetti-sullintelligenza-artificiale/2559226/)*

item.title: Workers beyond retirement age in Ticino: +220% in 20 years
item.description: In 2025, 220,000 people in Switzerland were working beyond retirement age. Here's what changes for cross-border workers in the Canton of Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- In 2025, 220,000 Swiss workers were over 65 years old
- The share of employed over 64 has increased from 2.1% to 4.5% in 20 years
- 12% of those over 65 are professionally active

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in workers of pensionable age
- When: From 2005 to 2025
- Where: Switzerland, with impact on Ticino and cross-border workers
- Who: Federal Statistical Office (UST) and Awp Agency
- Amount: 220,000 workers over 65 in 2025 vs 87,000 in 2005

In 2025, around 220,000 people in Switzerland continued to work after reaching the ordinary retirement age. This data, which emerges from an analysis by the Awp agency on the data of the Swiss Labor Force Survey (RIFOS) of the Federal Statistical Office (UST), represents more than double the 87,000 recorded in 2005. The share of workers over 64 among all employees has increased from 2.1% to 4.5%, with one in twenty workers being at least 65 years old.

### The demographic transformation
The trend reflects the demographic transformation of the country. The so-called baby boomers, born during a period of strong population growth, are entering the post-retirement age group, making the group of over 64 numerically larger. However, general behavior is also changing: more and more people choose to remain active in the labor market even after formal retirement. Currently, 12% of people aged 65 and over are employed, double the 7% recorded in 2005.

### Forms of employment
The phenomenon affects both men and women similarly. Among those who continue to work, more than half are self-employed and the vast majority work part-time. This change has significant implications for the labor market, especially in regions like the Canton of Ticino, where the presence of cross-border workers is high.

### Impact on cross-border workers
For cross-border workers in Ticino, the decision to continue working beyond 65 can have tax and pension implications. It is essential to understand the implications of Swiss laws, such as AHV and LPP, and how they interact with the Italian system, especially in terms of double taxation and returns.

### What to do if you decide to continue working
If a cross-border worker decides to continue working beyond 65, it is important to follow some steps:
1. Consult a tax expert: To understand the implications of double taxation and how to optimize returns.
2. Check AVS and LPP contributions: Ensure that the necessary contributions are continued to maintain pension rights.
3. Update the work permit: If necessary, renew the G permit or other required documents.
4. Evaluate insurance coverage: Check if it is necessary to adjust health insurance policies, such as LAMal, to cover any new risks related to age.

### Comparisons and scenarios
Comparing the current situation with that of 20 years ago, it is clear how the Swiss labor market is evolving. The choice to continue working beyond 65 is becoming more common, and this trend could have a significant impact on future pension and tax policies. For cross-border workers, it is essential to be well informed to make conscious decisions.

### Comparative tables

| Year | Workers over 65 | Share of total employees |
|------|---------------------|-------------------------|
| 2005 | 87,000 | 2.1% |
| 2025 | 220,000 | 4.5% |

| Gender | Share of workers over 65 | Increase since 2005 |
|--------|--------------------------|----------------------------|
| Men | 12% | +5% |
| Women | 12% | +5% |

| Type of employment | Percentage |
|------------------|-------------|
| Employees | 50% |
| Self-employed | 50% |
| Full-time | 30% |
| Part-time | 70% | ### Analysis of implications for cross-border workers
The increase in workers of retirement age in Switzerland has several implications for cross-border workers employed in Ticino. In particular, it is important to consider the tax and pension aspects, especially in terms of double taxation and returns.

### Double taxation and returns
Double taxation is a crucial issue for cross-border workers. In Switzerland, cross-border workers are subject to withholding tax, which is deducted directly from their salary. However, it is possible to benefit from bilateral agreements between Italy and Switzerland to avoid double taxation. It is essential to consult a tax expert to understand how to optimize returns and minimize the tax impact.

### AHV and BVG
The AHV (Old-Age and Survivors' Insurance) and BVG (Occupational Pension Provision) are two fundamental pillars of the Swiss pension system. For cross-border workers, it is important to continue paying AHV contributions even after the age of 65 to maintain pension rights. The BVG, on the other hand, is a professional pension system that can offer additional benefits, but it is necessary to verify the specific conditions of your employment contract.

### Insurance coverage
With age, health risks also increase. It is therefore important to check whether your health insurance policy, such as the LAMal (Mandatory Health Insurance), adequately covers your new needs. In some cases, it may be necessary to adjust the coverage or take out supplementary policies.

### Procedures to continue working
If a cross-border worker decides to continue working beyond the age of 65, there are several procedures to follow:
1. **Renewal of the work permit**: Check if it is necessary to renew the G permit or other required documents.
2. Update of pension contributions: Ensure that you continue to pay AHV and BVG contributions.
3. Consultation with a tax expert: To optimize the tax situation and benefit from any bilateral agreements.
4. Verification of insurance coverage: Adjust health insurance policies according to new needs.

### Future scenarios
The trend of continuing to work beyond the age of 65 is set to grow. This could have a significant impact on future pension and tax policies. For cross-border workers, it is essential to be well informed to make informed decisions and plan their future adequately.

### Comparison with the previous situation
20 years ago, the share of workers over 64 was 2.1%. Today, this percentage has doubled, reaching 4.5%. This change reflects not only the demographic transformation but also a change in people's behavior, who increasingly choose to remain active in the labor market even after formal retirement.

### Comparative tables

| Year | Share of workers over 64 | Percentage increase |
|------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| 2005 | 2.1% | - |
| 2025 | 4.5% | +2.4% |

| Gender | Share of workers over 65 | Increase compared to 2005 |
|--------|--------------------------|----------------------------|
| Men | 12% | +5% |
| Women | 12% | +5% |

| Type of employment | Percentage |
|------------------|-------------|
| Employees | 50% |
| Self-employed | 50% |
| Full-time | 30% |
| Part-time | 70% |

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
If you are a cross-border worker in Ticino and are considering continuing to work beyond the age of 65, here are some concrete steps to follow:

### 1. Consult a tax expert
Double taxation is a complex issue, but with the help of a tax expert, it is possible to optimize returns and minimize the tax impact. A consultant can help you understand how to benefit from the bilateral agreements between Italy and Switzerland and plan your tax situation effectively.

### 2. Update your pension contributions
It is essential to continue paying AVS and LPP contributions even after the age of 65 to maintain your pension rights. Check with your employer or a pension consultant to adjust your contributions according to your specific situation.

### 3. Renew your work permit
If necessary, renew your work permit, such as the G permit, to continue working legally in Switzerland. Make sure you have all the documents in order and respect the deadlines.

### 4. Verify your insurance coverage
With age, health risks increase. Verify if your health insurance, such as LAMal, adequately covers your new needs. You may need to adjust the coverage or subscribe to additional policies to ensure complete protection.

### 5. Plan for the future
Planning for the future is essential for making informed decisions. Consider your financial, pension, and health needs and plan accordingly. A financial consultant can help you create a personalized plan to ensure a peaceful future.

### Useful tools
To help you manage your situation effectively, you can use various tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website:
- Salary calculator: To estimate your net income after taxes.
- Insurance comparator: To find the health insurance policy that best suits your needs.
- **Guide to double taxation**: To understand how to optimize returns and minimize the tax impact.

### Final CTA
If you need further information or assistance, do not hesitate to contact us. We are here to help you navigate the complex world of cross-border work and ensure that you can enjoy all the benefits provided by law. Use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate your net income and plan your future effectively.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/economia/1921203/eta-anni-lavoratori-svizzera-aver)*

item.title: Family doctor reform: Sumai, the issue is organization and lack of professionals
item.description: Antonio Magi, general secretary of Sumai-Assoprof, contests the idea of dependency of family doctors, highlighting organizational problems and lack of professionals.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Sumai-Assoprof challenges the reform of general practitioners
- The issue is organizational, not contractual
- Risks: loss of trust relationship, waiting lists, higher costs

## Key facts
- **What**: Reform of general practitioners
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Sumai-Assoprof
- Amount: Not specified

Antonio Magi, general secretary of Sumai-Assoprof, the union of outpatient specialists, challenged the idea that the reform of general practitioners should focus on dependency. According to Magi, the main problem is not the contract but the organization and the shortage of professionals. 'It is not necessary to change the legal relationship of doctors, but to apply existing agreements, clarify roles and respect times,' Magi declared. The knot, according to him, is organizational: insufficient planning and shortage of professionals.

The proposal to introduce forms of dependency in general medicine is presented as a modern and efficient solution. However, Magi warns that it could produce effects opposite to those declared. Among the risks indicated: the loss of the trust relationship between doctor and patient, the creation of waiting lists to access the general practitioner and an exponential increase in healthcare costs.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The reform could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Lombardy. The loss of the trust relationship between doctor and patient could make it more difficult for cross-border workers to access care, who often find themselves having to manage their own health between two different healthcare systems. Furthermore, the creation of waiting lists could worsen the situation, making it even more complicated for cross-border workers to access necessary care in a timely manner.

### Comparison with the current situation

Currently, the contracted model guarantees immediate access to care and real care. The transition to a scheduled system, bound by shifts, objectives and organized activities, would inevitably introduce a time filter. This means that citizens, including cross-border workers, would no longer have direct access to the doctor, but would enter a regulated path, moving from a proximity system to a waiting system.

### Possible scenarios

If the reform goes ahead as proposed, cross-border workers may find themselves having to face longer waiting times for medical visits, with consequent delays in diagnoses and treatments. Furthermore, the increase in healthcare costs could lead to an increase in taxes or health contributions, with a direct impact on the wallets of cross-border workers.

### What to do

Cross-border workers who are concerned about the impact of the reform on their health and their wallet should closely monitor the development of the situation. It is important to stay informed and participate in public debates to make their voices heard. Furthermore, cross-border workers should consider the possibility of taking out complementary health insurance to ensure timely access to medical care. ## Practical Analysis

The proposed reform of general practitioners could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Lombardy. The loss of the trust relationship between doctor and patient could make it more difficult for cross-border workers to access healthcare, as they often have to manage their health between two different healthcare systems. Additionally, the creation of waiting lists could worsen the situation, making it even more complicated for cross-border workers to access necessary care in a timely manner.

### Comparison with the Current Situation

Currently, the contracted model guarantees immediate access to care and real follow-up. The transition to a scheduled system, bound by shifts, objectives, and organized activities, would inevitably introduce a time filter. This means that citizens, including cross-border workers, would no longer have direct access to the doctor but would enter a regulated path, moving from a proximity system to a waiting system.

### Possible Scenarios

If the reform goes ahead as proposed, cross-border workers could find themselves facing longer waiting times for medical visits, with consequent delays in diagnoses and treatments. Furthermore, the increase in healthcare costs could lead to higher taxes or health contributions, directly impacting the wallets of cross-border workers.

### What to Do

Cross-border workers who are concerned about the impact of the reform on their health and wallet should closely monitor the development of the situation. It is important to stay informed and participate in public debates to make their voices heard. Additionally, cross-border workers should consider the possibility of taking out supplementary health insurance to ensure timely access to medical care.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete action

For cross-border workers who want to ensure timely access to medical care, it is crucial to closely monitor the development of the reform. Participating in public debates and making your voice heard can make a difference. Additionally, taking out supplementary health insurance can provide greater peace of mind and security.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. **Stay informed**: Keep up to date with the latest news regarding the reform of general practitioners.
2. Participate: Join public debates and consultations to express your concerns and suggestions.
3. Insure: Consider taking out supplementary health insurance to ensure timely access to medical care.
4. Plan: Plan medical visits in advance to avoid prolonged waiting times.

### Useful tools

To calculate the financial impact of supplementary health insurance, you can use the [health insurance calculator](nav:health). This tool can help you compare the different options available and find the solution best suited to your needs.

### Conclusion

The reform of general practitioners could have a significant impact on cross-border workers. However, with adequate planning and the adoption of preventive measures, it is possible to mitigate risks and ensure timely access to medical care. Closely monitoring the development of the situation and participating in public debates are fundamental steps to make your voice heard and influence future decisions.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/27/leggi-notizia/articolo/riforma-medici-famiglia-sumai-nodo-non-e-contratto-ma-organizzazione-e-carenza-professionisti.html)*

item.title: Massive deployment of the Guardia di Finanza in Como: 36 patrols and 77 officers
item.description: Intense checks in Como: undocumented workers and seized drugs. Here are the details of the operation.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 36 patrols and 77 Guardia di Finanza officers in Como
- Undeclared workers found in the catering sector
- Over 2 grams of hashish seized in Alto Lario

## Key facts
- **What**: Guardia di Finanza checks
- When: Weekend of April 27, 2026
- Where: Province of Como
- Who: Guardia di Finanza of Como
- Amount: 3 undeclared workers found

A weekend of intense checks in the provincial area of Como saw the deployment of 36 patrols and 77 Guardia di Finanza officers. During the operation, 150 vehicles, 226 people, and 7 commercial establishments were checked. In Alto Lario, the Menaggio Company and the Oria Tenancy carried out targeted interventions against drug trafficking, reporting 2 individuals to the Prefecture and seizing over 2 grams of hashish. In Olgiatese, an individual was stopped with 2 seized joints. These checks follow similar operations in Erbese, where an individual was reported to the judicial authorities for possession of 12.60 grams of hashish and 997 CHF in cash.

### Discoveries in the catering sector

Officers from the Como Group and the Erba Company checked 4 commercial establishments, identifying 3 undeclared workers in the catering sector. This led to the request for suspension of activity by the Territorial Labor Inspectorate. Additionally, 1 irregular worker was discovered, and 2 fines were issued for irregularities in telematic transactions.

### Implications for cross-border workers

These intensified checks can have a significant impact on cross-border workers in the catering sector. It is essential to ensure compliance with local regulations to avoid sanctions and activity suspensions. Workers should verify their employment situation and ensure they respect all applicable laws.

### Procedures for employers

Employers must be particularly careful in verifying the regularity of their employees. It is advisable to conduct periodic checks and maintain updated documentation to avoid problems with the authorities. Additionally, it is important to be transparent with telematic transactions to avoid fines and sanctions.

### Comparisons with the previous situation

Compared to previous checks, this operation saw a significant increase in the number of patrols and officers involved. This indicates greater attention from the authorities towards combating undeclared work and drug trafficking. Cross-border workers must be aware of this trend and adopt preventive measures to avoid problems.

### Future scenarios

With the increase in checks, it is likely that the authorities will continue to closely monitor the territory. Cross-border workers and employers must be prepared for further inspections and ensure they respect all regulations. It is advisable to stay updated on the latest news and local laws to avoid sanctions and suspensions.

### Useful tools

To verify the regularity of their employment situation, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to ensure they respect all regulations. Additionally, it is useful to consult the guides and tools available on the site to stay updated on the latest news. ## Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers
