item.title: Nursing care: 190,000 signatures for full implementation of the initiative
item.description: 190,000 signatures handed in to Bern for the immediate application of the initiative on nursing care. The National Council discusses the new law.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 190,000 signatures for the immediate application of the nursing care initiative
- The National Council discusses the new federal law on working conditions
- Requests: reduction in working hours, better supplements, funding for measures

## Key facts
- **What**: Federal popular initiative "For strong nursing care"
- When: 27 April 2026
- Where: Bern
- Who: Eight trade union entities, including the Swiss Trade Union Federation, Unia, Syna
- Amount: 190,000 signatures

The federal popular initiative "For strong nursing care", approved by the people in 2021, must be fully and immediately implemented. This is the urgent appeal signed by 190,000 people and handed over on Monday in Bern to numerous federal parliamentarians. On Tuesday and Wednesday, the National Council will discuss the new federal law on working conditions in the nursing sector. The legislation implements the constitutional amendment that received support from 61% of voters and for which the care personnel have been fighting for years.

"However, the competent commission has made radical cuts to the already insufficient bill of the Federal Council," reads the note. "If the parliament approves this bill, the nursing initiative will remain without any effect: it would be a blatant disregard for the will expressed by the people."

To give more strength to their action, on Monday at noon on the Federal Square about 200 employees of the sector displayed a large banner, signs and a giant heart to remind the deputies of their responsibilities within the framework of the debate on their working conditions. The banner read: "190,000 people will hold you accountable."

Concretely, the healthcare personnel and the 190,000 signatories demand the full implementation of Article 117b of the Federal Constitution and a substantial correction of the law, introducing a significant reduction in working hours, a key for the distribution of personnel that allows quality care and better supplements for night work, weekends and public holidays. Another request: maintaining the obligation for all employers to respect at least the cantonal laws and collective agreements, as well as defining the necessary funding for all these measures for the health sector.

### The protest in Bern

The banner displayed on the Federal Square read: "190,000 people will hold you accountable." The protest was organized by eight trade union entities, including the Swiss Trade Union Federation, Unia, Syna and Travail.Suisse. The demonstration saw the participation of about 200 employees of the sector, who displayed signs and a giant heart to draw the attention of the deputies to their working conditions.

### The requests of the healthcare personnel

The healthcare personnel and the signatories demand the full implementation of Article 117b of the Federal Constitution. Among the main requests are:
- A significant reduction in working hours
- A key for the distribution of personnel that allows quality care
- Better supplements for night work, weekends and public holidays
- Maintaining the obligation for employers to respect cantonal laws and collective agreements
- Defining the necessary funding for all these measures for the health sector

In a resolution voted on the Federal Square on the occasion of the national demonstration on 22 November 2025, the personnel of the sector committed to resorting to every measure of struggle, including strike, to make their voice heard. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The demands of healthcare staff and signatories could have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in the nursing care sector in Switzerland. A reduction in working hours and improved working conditions could attract more qualified personnel, improving the quality of care and reducing the workload.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the approval of the popular initiative in 2021, working conditions in the nursing care sector were already critical. A lack of staff and excessive workloads were common problems. With the approval of the initiative, a window of opportunity opened to improve these conditions. However, the cuts made by the competent commission could undermine these efforts.

### Possible scenarios

If the Parliament approves the bill with the cuts made by the commission, the initiative on care will remain ineffective. This could lead to an increase in protests and industrial actions by healthcare staff, including strikes. On the other hand, if the Parliament accepts the demands of healthcare staff, significant improvements in working conditions and the quality of care could be seen.

### Impact on the Canton of Ticino

In the Canton of Ticino, where the nursing care sector is particularly relevant, improvements in working conditions could attract more qualified personnel, reducing staff shortages and improving the quality of care. This could also have a positive impact on cross-border workers in the sector, offering them better opportunities and working conditions.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do now

For cross-border workers in the nursing care sector, it is important to closely monitor the developments in the National Council discussions. If the demands of the healthcare staff are accepted, there may be new opportunities and improvements in working conditions.

### Procedure for cross-border workers

1. **Monitor the news**: Stay updated on the discussions in the National Council and the decisions regarding the new federal law on working conditions in the nursing care sector.
2. Participate in protests: If interested, join the demonstrations and protest actions organized by healthcare staff to make your voice heard.
3. Contact the unions: Reach out to unions like Unia, Syna, and Travail.Suisse for information and support on your working conditions.
4. Evaluate opportunities: If working conditions improve, consider new job opportunities in the nursing care sector in Switzerland.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to deepen their knowledge about working conditions and opportunities in the nursing care sector, the following tools may be useful:
- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator)
- [Salary comparison](nav:salary-compare)
- [Guides for cross-border workers](nav:jobs)

### Final CTA

For more information and to calculate your net salary in Switzerland, use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3696831)*

item.title: A job position only for cross-border workers: 'Enough, it's scandalous'
item.description: Fabrizio Sirica denounces a job posting in Massagno exclusively reserved for cross-border workers with a gross salary of 2,900 francs per month.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Job announcement in Massagno reserved exclusively for cross-border workers
- Salary of 2,900 francs gross per month
- Fabrizio Sirica calls for a strategy for resident workers

## Key facts
- **What**: Job announcement reserved for cross-border workers
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Massagno, Canton Ticino
- Who: Fabrizio Sirica, Grand Councilor
- Amount: 2,900 francs gross per month

Grand Councilor Fabrizio Sirica has once again made headlines with a new complaint regarding a job announcement in Massagno. The announcement, exclusively for cross-border workers, offers a fixed-term administrative position with an annual salary of 34,800 francs, equivalent to 2,900 francs gross per month. Sirica described the situation as 'scandalous' and submitted a motion to the State Council to request a strategy in favor of resident workers.

Sirica reported the case to the inspectorate, highlighting the ambiguity of the announcement that mentions an 80% position in one place and an 80/100% position in another. 'It is evident that a resident cannot live on a salary of 2,900 francs,' the Grand Councilor stated. The position requires excellent command of the English language and digital tools, as well as the ability to manage work independently.

### Context and Reactions

This is not the first case reported by Sirica. In recent months, the Grand Councilor has collected several reports of job announcements with poverty wages or inappropriate requests. The Cantonal Parliament recently approved a compromise that will set the minimum wage between 21.75 and 22.25 francs per hour, equivalent to approximately 4,000 francs gross per month for full-time work. The measure is expected to come into effect by 2029.

Sirica expressed his concern about the lack of a strategy to favor Ticinese people and those who have chosen to move to Switzerland. 'I don't have it in for cross-border workers. But we need to protect those who live here,' he declared.

### Practical Implications

The announcement in Massagno raises several questions about working conditions and discrimination based on residence. Sirica requested clear and verifiable quantitative objectives for the professional integration of residents, with multi-year planning. Additionally, he asked for an annual monitoring system with public indicators by economic sector.

### Next Steps

Sirica submitted a motion to the State Council requesting concrete measures. Among the requests is the development of sectoral recruitment plans, particularly in sectors characterized by a high presence of cross-border workers and low wages. He also requested targeted support measures to facilitate the integration of residents into positions made available or accessible by the new salary framework.

To delve deeper into the practical implications and procedures to follow, consult our [salary calculator](nav:calculator). ### Context Analysis

The job announcement in Massagno, exclusively reserved for cross-border workers, raises several questions about working conditions and discrimination based on residence. Fabrizio Sirica reported the case to the inspectorate, highlighting the ambiguity of the announcement that mentions an 80% position in one part and an 80/100% position in another. This type of announcement not only offers inadequate wages but also working conditions that can be considered discriminatory.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

In recent months, Sirica has collected several reports of job advertisements with starvation wages or inappropriate requests. This case is just the latest in a series of reports that highlight the difficulties faced by resident workers in finding dignified employment. The cantonal parliament recently approved a compromise that will bring the minimum wage to a range between 21.75 and 22.25 francs per hour, i.e., approximately 4,000 francs gross per month for full-time work. The measure is expected to come into force by 2029.

### Future Scenarios

Sirica has requested clear and verifiable quantitative objectives for the professional integration of residents, with a multi-year time planning. Additionally, he asked for an annual monitoring system with public indicators per economic sector. These measures could help ensure that residents have the same job opportunities as cross-border workers.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

While Sirica has clarified that he has nothing against cross-border workers, it is important to consider the implications for resident workers. The announcement in Massagno raises questions about working conditions and discrimination based on residence. It is crucial that the cantonal authorities adopt concrete measures to ensure that all workers, regardless of their residence, have access to dignified working conditions.

For more information on procedures and deadlines, consult our [guide to work permits](nav:permits).

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to Do if You're a Resident Worker

If you're a resident worker in Ticino and you've come across a job advertisement that you believe is discriminatory, you can follow these steps:

1. **Gather Evidence**: Keep a copy of the advertisement and any other communication related to the job.
2. Contact the Labor Inspectorate: Report the case to the Labor Inspectorate of the Canton of Ticino. Provide all necessary information and the evidence you've gathered.
3. Consult a Legal Expert: If necessary, consult a lawyer specializing in labor law to get legal assistance.
4. Participate in Local Initiatives: Join local initiatives and discussion forums to stay updated on the latest news and to share your experiences with other workers.

### Useful Tools

To help you navigate the complex world of work in Ticino, you can use the following tools:

- Salary Calculator: Use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of how much you can earn based on your qualifications and experience.
- Guide to Work Permits: Consult our [guide to work permits](nav:permits) to understand which permits are necessary to work in Ticino.
- Legal Assistance: If you need legal assistance, consult a lawyer specializing in labor law.

### Conclusion

The job advertisement in Massagno, exclusively reserved for cross-border workers, raises important questions about working conditions and discrimination based on residence. It is crucial that the cantonal authorities take concrete measures to ensure that all workers, regardless of their residence, have access to dignified working conditions. If you are a resident worker and have found a job advertisement that you believe is discriminatory, report it to the labor inspectorate and consult a legal expert if necessary. Use our tools to stay updated and get assistance.

For more information, consult our [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921176/un-posto-di-lavoro-solo-per-frontalieri-basta-e-scandaloso)*

item.title: Ticinese volunteers on humanitarian mission in Ukraine: the seventh mission of HUNPA
item.description: Ticinese volunteers are preparing to bring medical assistance and comfort to the Ukrainian population affected by the war.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Ticinese volunteers on humanitarian mission in Ukraine
- Specific training to operate in war zones
- Seventh mission for the HUNPA association

## Key facts
- **What**: Humanitarian mission in Ukraine
- When: Weekend, unspecified date
- Where: Ronco Bedretto, Ticino
- Who: HUNPA association
- Number: Between 4 and 6 volunteers

The Ticinese volunteer association Humanitarian Nursing and Paramedical Aid (HUNPA) has trained its new volunteers in preparation for their seventh mission in Ukraine. This weekend, in Ronco Bedretto, the volunteers prepared to provide medical assistance and comfort to the Ukrainian population affected by the conflict. The mere presence of the volunteers brings relief, in addition to the distribution of essential goods, food, and medicines. Yves Toutounghi, vice-president of HUNPA, explains that "when they see us arrive, the first thing they tell us is that they don't feel abandoned." The association's goal, since 2003, has been to provide healthcare in disadvantaged areas suffering the effects of war. The volunteers visit people, help with health needs, and distribute medicines, also bringing a gesture of solidarity.

### Motivations and training
Cristiano Fusetti, one of the volunteers, recounts having always been interested in humanitarian volunteering since his youth, when he approached the world of the Red Cross. Specific training for future missions is fundamental. "If I know how to protect myself and if I reflect on what I need to do, I've done 50% of the work," emphasizes Toutounghi. The preparation includes learning field dynamics; after theoretical training, the volunteers will "go into the field" to understand how to move strategically, what to observe, and how to behave in a group. Situational awareness is crucial: knowing where you are and never letting your guard down while operating in a dangerous context.

### Preparation and departure
The volunteers departing will be between four and six, according to Gianluca Ugolini, president of HUNPA. The mission will focus on disadvantaged areas, visiting people and helping with their health needs. Specific training is essential to safeguard one's integrity and understand field dynamics. After theoretical training, the volunteers will have their first contact with the field, learning to move strategically and observe the surrounding environment.

### Implications for cross-border workers
This humanitarian mission not only brings medical assistance and comfort to the Ukrainian population but also strengthens the ties between the Ticinese community and war zones. Volunteers, also from other regions, contribute to a sense of solidarity and international cooperation. Specific training and field preparation are fundamental to operate safely and effectively, ensuring the necessary support to the affected populations.

### Conclusion
HUNPA's mission in Ukraine represents an example of humanitarian commitment and international solidarity. The Ticinese volunteers, with their training and preparation, are ready to bring assistance and comfort to populations affected by war. This initiative not only helps people in difficulty directly but also strengthens the values of cooperation and mutual support among communities.

For more information and to discover how to participate in future missions, visit the official HUNPA website or contact the association for more details. ## Practical Analysis: Challenges and Opportunities
The humanitarian mission in Ukraine presents a significant challenge for Ticinese volunteers. Operating in a war zone requires specific preparation and strong situational awareness. Volunteers must be ready to face difficult conditions and provide medical assistance and comfort to the local population. Theoretical and practical training is fundamental to ensure the safety and effectiveness of field operations.

### Challenges in the Field
War zones present numerous risks and challenges for volunteers. Situational awareness is crucial for operating safely. Volunteers must be able to constantly assess the surrounding environment and quickly adapt to emergency situations. Specific training includes learning field dynamics, such as moving strategically, observing the environment, and behaving in groups. These elements are essential to ensure the safety of volunteers and the effectiveness of operations.

### Opportunities for Solidarity
The humanitarian mission also offers opportunities for solidarity and international cooperation. Ticinese volunteers, along with those from other regions, contribute to strengthening ties between communities and promoting values of mutual support. The presence of volunteers brings comfort and hope to affected populations, demonstrating that they are not alone in facing the difficulties of war.

### Conclusion
The HUNPA mission in Ukraine is an example of humanitarian commitment and international solidarity. Ticinese volunteers, with their training and preparation, are ready to provide assistance and comfort to populations affected by war. This initiative not only directly helps people in need but also strengthens the values of cooperation and mutual support between communities.

For more information and to discover how to participate in future missions, visit the official HUNPA website or contact the association for more details.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions: how to participate and support
If you are interested in participating in humanitarian missions with HUNPA or supporting the association, there are several concrete actions you can take. The first thing is to visit HUNPA's official website to get detailed information about future missions and the requirements to become a volunteer. The association offers specific training and support to prepare volunteers for field challenges.

### Steps to become a volunteer
1. **Inform yourself**: Visit HUNPA's official website to learn about current missions and the requirements to become a volunteer.
2. Contact the association: For more details, contact HUNPA directly to get information about upcoming missions and the selection processes.
3. Attend training: Once selected, participate in the theoretical and practical training offered by the association. This training is fundamental to operating safely and effectively in the field.
4. Prepare for the mission: After training, prepare for the mission with the help of the association. This includes fundraising, preparing the necessary materials, and logistical planning.
5. Participate in the mission: Once ready, participate in the humanitarian mission and bring assistance and comfort to the affected populations.

### How to support HUNPA
If you cannot participate directly in the missions, you can still support HUNPA in various ways. Financial donations, in-kind contributions, and local volunteering are all ways to contribute to the association's mission. Visit HUNPA's official website to discover how you can help and make a difference.

### Conclusion
HUNPA's mission in Ukraine represents an example of humanitarian commitment and international solidarity. The Ticino volunteers, with their training and preparation, are ready to bring assistance and comfort to the populations affected by the war. This initiative not only helps people in need directly but also strengthens the values of cooperation and mutual support among communities.

For more information and to discover how to participate in future missions, visit HUNPA's official website or contact the association for more details.

item.title: Pornographic deepfakes: what Swiss law says
item.description: Switzerland does not have a specific offense for pornographic deepfakes, but existing law can be applied in particular cases
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Pornographic deepfakes created with AI in Switzerland
- Eight women have filed a complaint
- No specific crime, but laws on identity and honor

## Key facts
- **What**: Pornographic deepfakes created with artificial intelligence
- When: Contents circulated for months on Telegram
- Where: Switzerland, with at least 14 women involved
- Who: Eight women have filed a complaint
- Amount: Not specified

Switzerland is not immune to the problem of pornographic deepfakes, contents created with artificial intelligence that superimpose the face of a real person onto another body in images or videos with sexual content. According to an investigation by Tages-Anzeiger, for months on Telegram there have been deepfakes of Swiss influencers. At least 14 women are involved, eight of whom have filed a complaint. The channels also exchanged instructions on how to create these sexual fakes in a simple and quick way.

For Angela Müller, of Algorithm Watch, the case demonstrates that pornographic deepfakes are not a marginal phenomenon. 'The problem is wider than a few isolated episodes,' says Müller. Sexism and sexualized violence online are certainly not new, but AI acts as a multiplier. The organization therefore calls for social media platforms and providers of artificial intelligence tools to be called upon to take greater responsibility.

### Laws in Switzerland

In Switzerland, there is no specific criminal offense related to the production and dissemination of pornographic deepfakes. However, the Federal Council considers that the existing law is, in principle, applicable to this type of content. Depending on the cases, provisions already foreseen in the Criminal Code, such as identity theft or crimes against honor, may come into play. The unauthorized dissemination of intimate content can also have criminal relevance. 'In my opinion, the existing norms already cover several aspects of the problem,' explains Manuel Bertschi, a lawyer specializing in communication law. Fundamental, he emphasizes, is that justice applies them consistently also to cases of pornographic deepfakes.

A problem of application

According to Bertschi, the central issue is the application of the law. The people affected, in fact, often do not know who is behind the anonymous accounts that spread these contents. To proceed criminally, however, it is necessary to identify a natural or legal person to pursue. 'I support the need to introduce by law a responsibility of the platforms,' says the lawyer. Currently, he explains, one of the main problems is precisely the fact that the platforms do not respond legally for the publication of deepfakes. Angela Müller shares this position: in her opinion, the platforms should be called to respond when they are aware of illegal content but do not proceed to remove it.

### What to do against pornographic deepfakes?

The priority is to document the contents and report them to the platforms to obtain their removal. If the author is known and the goal is to quickly remove the material, lawyer Bertschi advises taking action in civil court. In parallel, a criminal complaint can also be filed, for example for identity theft or crimes against honor.

Bans under discussion

In the National Council, several motions are pending that call for a ban on so-called 'nudification' apps. These are applications that allow easily creating deepfakes from existing photos of a person. The problem with banning the applications is that these can change name, web address or technical infrastructure. After the blocking of a site or service, often another similar one quickly appears.

A social problem

Bans and new laws are therefore only part of the solution, according to Angela Müller, who does not consider a discussion limited to criminal law sufficient. When these contents start circulating online, she points out, the damage has often already been done. Prevention measures, reference points for the people affected and greater responsibility on the part of the platforms, whose design and algorithms can favor the spread of problematic contents, are therefore also needed. Pornographic deepfakes, concludes Müller, are the expression of a broader social problem, which cannot be solved only with laws. ### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy may be particularly vulnerable to pornographic deepfakes, given their frequent use of digital platforms and social media. The lack of specific legislation in Switzerland could make it more difficult to protect victims, especially if the authors of the deepfakes operate from different jurisdictions.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the emergence of pornographic deepfakes, victims of revenge porn or unauthorized dissemination of intimate content could rely on existing regulations such as identity theft and crimes against honor. However, deepfake technology has made it more complex to apply these norms, as it is often difficult to identify the authors and prove the intentionality of the harm.

### Concrete scenarios

Imagine a cross-border worker residing in Ticino and working in Italy. If they become a victim of a pornographic deepfake, they might face several challenges. First, they would need to document the content and report it to the platforms to have it removed. If the author is known, they could take civil action to have the material quickly deleted. In parallel, they could file a criminal complaint for identity theft or crimes against honor.

### Platform responsibility

A crucial aspect is the responsibility of social media platforms. Currently, platforms are not legally responsible for the publication of deepfakes, which makes it more difficult for victims to obtain justice. Angela Müller and Manuel Bertschi argue that platforms should be held accountable when they are aware of illegal content but do not take steps to remove it.

### Preventive measures

In addition to laws and legal actions, preventive measures are necessary. Platforms could implement more effective algorithms to detect and remove pornographic deepfakes. Furthermore, reference points could be created for affected individuals, such as psychological and legal support centers. Greater awareness of the problem among users could also help prevent the spread of such content.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do if you are a victim of pornographic deepfake

If you are a victim of a pornographic deepfake, the first thing to do is to document the content. Take screenshots or save the videos and report them to the platforms to have them removed. If you know the author, you can take civil action to have the material quickly removed. In parallel, you can file a criminal complaint for identity theft or crimes against honor.

### Reporting to platforms

Each platform has its own reporting system. For example, on Facebook and Instagram, you can report inappropriate content through the post or profile options menu. On Telegram, you can report a channel or message through the channel or message options menu.

### Criminal complaint

If you decide to file a criminal complaint, you can contact the local police or a lawyer specializing in communication law. The lawyer can help you gather evidence and file the complaint. Remember that it is important to act quickly, as the content may be removed or modified over time.

### Psychological support

Being a victim of a pornographic deepfake can be a traumatic experience. It is important to seek psychological support to cope with the anxiety, shame, and fear that can result from this situation. You can turn to a therapist or a specialized support center.

### Useful tools

For further information and support, you can visit the Algorithm Watch website or contact a lawyer specializing in communication law. Additionally, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare your earnings and plan your finances.

### Conclusion

Pornographic deepfakes are a complex problem that requires a multifaceted approach. In addition to legal actions, preventive measures and greater responsibility from platforms are necessary. If you are a victim of a pornographic deepfake, do not hesitate to seek help and support. Use the available tools to document the content, report it to the platforms, and file a criminal complaint if necessary.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3686991)*

item.title: Nurses in short supply: surge in arrivals from Tunisia, India and Paraguay
item.description: The shortage of nurses in Italy is driving companies to recruit abroad, with over 1,000 professionals selected in the last three years.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Shortage of 1 million nurses in Europe by 2030
- Over 1,000 professionals selected in the last three years
- Lombardy and Piedmont are the most active regions

## Key facts
- **What**: Shortage of nurses and healthcare staff in Italy
- When: Current data and forecasts up to 2030
- Where: Italy, with a focus on Lombardy, Piedmont, Sardinia, Veneto, Calabria
- Who: Openjobmetis Spa, International Recruitment
- Amount: 1,000 professionals selected, 5,000 interviews abroad

The shortage of nurses and healthcare staff in Italy has become a real emergency. According to the World Health Organization, by 2030 Europe will be short of 1 million nurses. This critical situation has pushed recruitment companies to seek solutions abroad. Openjobmetis Spa, through its International Recruitment division, has selected and trained over 1,000 healthcare professionals in the last three years, coming from countries such as Tunisia, India, and Paraguay.

Lombardy is the region that has welcomed the largest number of these professionals, with 50% of activations, followed by Piedmont with 19%. Sardinia, Veneto, and Calabria complete the ranking of the regions most active in recruiting foreign healthcare staff. The motivations that push these professionals to move to Italy are mainly economic, with the possibility of improving professionally in our hospitals.

### Countries of origin and profile of professionals

The countries of origin of the selected professionals are mainly Tunisia (30%), India (25%), and Paraguay (15%), followed by Brazil, Peru, El Salvador, and others. The profile of the nurses hired is 85% represented by women, of whom 70% have children. The average age is between 25 and 38 years, with an average previous professional experience of 5 years.

The selection process involves an in-depth knowledge of the Italian language, the recognition of the academic title from the country of origin, and a one-month on-the-job training period. After the first two years, most of the professionals remain in Italy.

Daniela Pomarolli, head of International Recruitment at Openjobmetis, stated: 'Recently, we have been focusing on a new country, El Salvador, which allows us to attract numerous well-prepared nurses both linguistically and technically.' ## Implications for cross-border workers and the healthcare system

The shortage of healthcare personnel in Italy has a significant impact on cross-border workers in the sector. The presence of foreign professionals can influence the labor market dynamics, offering new opportunities but also creating competition. For cross-border workers in Ticino and neighboring regions, it is important to be aware of the new dynamics in the healthcare labor market.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this wave of recruitment abroad, Italian healthcare facilities mainly relied on local personnel and some professionals from European Union countries. The arrival of nurses and healthcare professionals from non-European countries represents a significant novelty. This change can lead to greater cultural diversity within healthcare facilities, with benefits in terms of knowledge and skill exchange.

### Future scenarios

With the increasing demand for healthcare personnel, it is likely that recruitment abroad will continue to grow. This could lead to greater integration of foreign professionals in the Italian healthcare system, with possible impacts on immigration policies and training requirements. For cross-border workers, it is important to monitor these developments to adapt to the new dynamics of the labor market.

### Comparative table: Countries of origin and percentages

| Country | Percentage |
|--------------|-------------|
| Tunisia | 30% |
| India | 25% |
| Paraguay | 15% |
| Brazil | 12% |
| Peru | 9% |
| El Salvador | 7% |
| Others | 2% |

This table shows the distribution of selected healthcare professionals based on their country of origin. Tunisia is the country with the highest percentage, followed by India and Paraguay.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in the healthcare sector in Ticino and neighboring regions, it is crucial to be aware of the new opportunities and challenges arising from the recruitment of healthcare personnel abroad. Here are some concrete actions they can take:

1. **Professional development**: Participate in training and refresher courses to enhance their skills and remain competitive in the job market.
2. Language learning: Improve their knowledge of the Italian language, which is a fundamental requirement for working in Italy.
3. Monitoring opportunities: Stay updated on new job opportunities and selection requirements for foreign professionals.
4. Cultural integration: Participate in cultural integration programs to foster collaboration and knowledge exchange with new foreign colleagues.

### Selection and placement procedure

The selection and placement process for foreign healthcare professionals involves several stages:

1. Initial selection: Interviews and assessment of linguistic and professional skills.
2. Recognition of qualifications: Verification and recognition of the qualifications from the country of origin.
3. On-the-job training: One-month training period to perfect Italian practices.
4. Market placement: Support in finding employment and integrating into Italian healthcare facilities.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who wish to explore job opportunities in the healthcare sector, it is useful to consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare salaries and working conditions in different Italian regions. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help understand the requirements and procedures for obtaining the necessary work permits.

In conclusion, the recruitment of nurses and healthcare professionals abroad is a response to the shortage of personnel in the sector. For cross-border workers, it is important to be aware of these dynamics and adapt to the new opportunities and challenges that arise.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/27/leggi-notizia/articolo/infermieri-introvabili-si-cercano-allestero-boom-di-arrivi-da-tunisia-india-e-paraguay.html)*

item.title: Meeting between solidarity and security in Bioggio
item.description: Volunteers from City Angels Switzerland share direct experiences on May 16th at the Faressere Center in Bioggio
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Meeting with City Angels Svizzera on May 16 in Bioggio
- Volunteers share experiences of solidarity and safety
- Event at Centro Faressere, via Campagna 32

## Key facts
- **What**: Meeting with volunteers of City Angels Svizzera
- When: May 16, 2026, from 17.30 to 19.00
- Where: Centro Faressere, via Campagna 32, Bioggio (Hangar 51, Box 34)
- Who: Volunteers of City Angels Svizzera and president Antonio Chiarella
- Cost: Not specified

On Saturday, May 16, from 17.30 to 19.00, at Centro Faressere, via Campagna 32 in Bioggio (Hangar 51, Box 34), a meeting with City Angels Svizzera will take place. During the evening, volunteers will share stories and direct experiences, offering the public a concrete insight into their daily commitment to solidarity and urban safety promotion. A moment of listening and reflection on what it means, concretely, to 'be there' for others. President Antonio Chiarella will intervene.

The meeting represents an opportunity to get to know those who operate daily in the territory in support of people in difficulty. This event is particularly relevant for the community of Bioggio and surrounding areas, where solidarity and safety are fundamental values. The volunteers of City Angels Svizzera, with their constant presence, contribute to creating a safer and more welcoming environment for all residents.

### The importance of solidarity and safety

Solidarity and urban safety are highly topical issues, especially in a context like that of Bioggio, where the community is strongly linked to values of mutual aid and reciprocal protection. The meeting on May 16 represents a unique opportunity to delve into these themes and better understand the work carried out by the volunteers of City Angels Svizzera.

### The role of City Angels Svizzera

City Angels Svizzera is an organization operating in the Swiss territory with the aim of promoting solidarity and urban safety. The volunteers of this association are daily engaged in activities of support to people in difficulty, offering concrete help and creating a sense of community among residents. The meeting on May 16 will be an opportunity to get to know their work better and understand the positive impact they have on the community.

### How to participate

The meeting is open to everyone and does not require any reservation. To participate, simply show up at Centro Faressere, via Campagna 32 in Bioggio, Hangar 51, Box 34, on Saturday, May 16 from 17.30 to 19.00. It will be an opportunity to listen to the direct experiences of the volunteers and reflect together on how to promote a more solidary and safe community. - ## Practical implications for the community

The meeting between solidarity and security in Bioggio represents an opportunity for the community to reflect on the importance of volunteering and mutual support. The volunteers of City Angels Svizzera, with their direct experiences, offer a concrete example of how it is possible to make a difference in the lives of people in difficulty.

### Solidarity as a fundamental value

Solidarity is a fundamental value for the community of Bioggio. The meeting on May 16 represents an opportunity to strengthen this value and promote a sense of belonging among residents. The volunteers of City Angels Svizzera, with their daily commitment, demonstrate how solidarity can make a difference in people's lives.

### Urban security as a priority

Urban security is a priority for the community of Bioggio. The meeting on May 16 represents an opportunity to discuss how to promote greater urban security and create a safer environment for all residents. The volunteers of City Angels Svizzera, with their direct experiences, offer concrete insights on how to improve urban security.

### The role of the community

The community of Bioggio has a fundamental role in promoting solidarity and urban security. The meeting on May 16 represents an opportunity to reflect on how the community can contribute to creating a safer and more welcoming environment for all residents. The volunteers of City Angels Svizzera, with their daily commitment, offer a concrete example of how the community can make a difference.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to participate

To participate in the meeting between solidarity and security in Bioggio, simply show up at the Centro Faressere, via Campagna 32, Hangar 51, Box 34, on Saturday, May 16th, from 5:30 PM to 7:00 PM. No registration is required, the event is open to everyone.

### How to get to Centro Faressere

Centro Faressere is located in Bioggio, via Campagna 32. To reach the center, you can use public transportation or a car. If you use a car, there is parking available near the center. If you use public transportation, the closest stop is Bioggio, a few minutes' walk from the center.

### What to bring

It is not necessary to bring anything special to participate in the meeting. However, it is advisable to bring a notebook and a pen to take notes during the volunteers' testimonies. Additionally, it is important to arrive a few minutes early to find a seat and prepare for listening.

### What to expect

During the meeting, the volunteers of City Angels Svizzera will share their direct experiences of solidarity and urban security. It will be an opportunity to hear concrete stories and reflect on how to promote a more solidarity and secure community. President Antonio Chiarella will intervene to introduce the topics of the meeting and moderate the discussion.

### After the meeting

After the meeting, it will be possible to stay in touch with the volunteers of City Angels Svizzera and participate in other initiatives of solidarity and urban security. Additionally, it will be possible to subscribe to the City Angels Svizzera newsletter to receive updates on events and activities of the association.

For further information, it is possible to visit the City Angels Svizzera website or contact the association through social media channels. Additionally, it is possible to use the [solidarity calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how to contribute to the community of Bioggio and surroundings.
